summary: Configure Docker Compose with MySQL 8 for local development infrastructure
description: Set up a shared MySQL 8 Docker Compose service for local development. This is an infrastructure-level story not tied to any single service. All backend services requiring MySQL will depend on this container. Configuration covers the Compose service definition, named volumes, networking, custom MySQL settings, a healthcheck, an init script to provision databases and users on first startup, and environment variable definitions in .env.example.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: MySQL container starts and passes healthcheck
acceptanceCriteria.given: a developer has Docker installed and has copied infra/.env.example to infra/.env
acceptanceCriteria.when: they run docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml up -d
acceptanceCriteria.then: the MySQL 8 container starts, the healthcheck passes, and the service is reachable on the configured port

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Databases and users are provisioned automatically on first startup
acceptanceCriteria.given: no prior MySQL data volume exists
acceptanceCriteria.when: the MySQL container starts for the first time
acceptanceCriteria.then: the init script runs, creating the required databases and users with the correct grants before any service attempts to connect

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Secrets are not committed to source control
acceptanceCriteria.then: .env is absent from git but .env.example is present with all required variable names and safe placeholder defaults

acceptanceCriteria.given: the MySQL container has been used and stopped
acceptanceCriteria.when: docker compose up is run again
acceptanceCriteria.then: previously written data is available because a named volume preserves the data directory

tasks.summary: Populate infra/.env.example with MySQL environment variables
tasks.description:
Add all MySQL-related variable definitions to infra/.env.example with safe placeholder defaults:

DATABASE_HOST=localhost
DATABASE_PORT=3306
DATABASE_ROOT_PASSWORD=rootpassword
DATABASE_USER=loanflow
DATABASE_PASSWORD=password
LOAN_SERVICE_DB=loanflow_loan_db

Add a comment block grouping these under # MySQL. This file is committed. The actual .env is covered by infra/.gitignore.

tasks.summary: Create infra/docker-compose.yml with MySQL 8 service definition
tasks.description:
Define the MySQL 8 service in infra/docker-compose.yml with the following configuration:

- Image: mysql:8.0
- Container name: loanflow-mysql
- env_file: .env
- Environment: MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD, MYSQL_USER, MYSQL_PASSWORD sourced from env vars
- Port mapping: ${DATABASE_PORT:-3306}:3306
- Named volume: mysql_data mounted to /var/lib/mysql
- Init script volume: ./mysql/init:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d (read-only)
- Custom config volume: ./mysql/conf.d:/etc/mysql/conf.d (read-only)
- Healthcheck: mysqladmin ping -h localhost -u root -p$$MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD with interval 10s, timeout 5s, retries 5
- Restart policy: unless-stopped

Declare the mysql_data named volume at the top-level volumes key.

tasks.summary: Create MySQL init script to provision databases and users

tasks.description:
Create infra/mysql/conf.d/custom.cnf to configure MySQL server settings beyond the defaults:

[mysqld]
character-set-server = utf8mb4
collation-server     = utf8mb4_unicode_ci
default-time-zone    = '+00:00'
max_connections      = 200
innodb_buffer_pool_size = 256M
slow_query_log       = 1
long_query_time      = 2

This file is mounted read-only into the container at /etc/mysql/conf.d.
